news_catalyst.headline: Broadcom Q3 AI guidance miss + weak 172K jobs report drives global semiconductor selloff
news_catalyst.summary: Two simultaneous catalysts drove today's selloff. First, Broadcom guided Q3 AI revenue at $16B — below the $17.3B consensus — and reiterated rather than raised its FY2027 $100B+ AI target. The market expected a raise; the reiterate was treated as a miss. Second, the US July 4 jobs report (172K payrolls, below expectations) eliminated near-term rate cut hopes and introduced rate hike risk, per CME FedWatch. Higher rates compress chip multiples aggressively. The global ripple: South Korea's Kospi fell 5.5%, SK Hynix -10%, Samsung -6.4%. ASML -3.8% in Europe.

technical.summary: MU has lost $280 from its $1,255 June 25 peak — over 22% in 10 days. The stair-step distribution pattern (Jun 26 -7%, Jul 1 -10.57%, today -5.49%) shows consistent overhead supply with no high-volume bounce. The $950.28 intraday low is the new critical support. The 1.08x volume on today's down day is not a capitulation flush — a genuine SC would show 2x+ volume with an intraday reversal. Until that pattern appears, distribution is assumed to be ongoing.

wyckoff_narrative: MU continues in an extended distribution phase post the June 25 peak. High-volume down days have not been met with equal-volume bounces. The absence of a true SC (2x+ volume + sharp reversal) means the selling has not exhausted. The $950 floor is the next key test; if it holds, watch for a potential SC setup in coming sessions.

valuation.summary: DCF $1,192 vs $975 = 18.2% MoS. Bear DCF at $690 represents -30% additional downside if AI capex moderates. Insufficient buffer to step in front of the technical downtrend.

risks: Global semiconductor peer selloff (SK Hynix -10%) signals macro problem not idiosyncratic
Rate hike risk re-entering market reduces chip multiple expansion potential
Bear DCF $690 is -30% from here if AI spending moderates
